mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >55% of triggered instances, or signal accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and producing negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal yields positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle demonstrates resilient corporate output that offsets rate hike fears, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of tightening periods with rising industrial production, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index falling below 205.0 signals disinflation that prompts expectations of central bank easing, lowering discount rates and boosting equity valuations over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when CPIAUCSL falls below the threshold, or predictive power vanishes after 2007.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and flat payrolls indicates labor market fragility that triggers recession fears, causing negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns with >50% accuracy over 20-day horizon, or interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product correlates with expanding corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon through fundamental valuation support.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for 60-day forward returns, or positive returns occur in <45% of growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 112.0 indicates a supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning in equities, generating negative returns over one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or threshold model yields negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward validation.
